description:
A routine post-backup audit needs to be run by a junior backup engineer (you).  
  The backup set already exists in:

      /home/user/backups

  1.  Verify every file in /home/user/backups against the reference checksum file  
      /home/user/backups/sha256sums.txt.

      • The verification **must** be done with SHA-256.  
      • Create a directory called /home/user/backup_audit (if it is not already there).  
      • Inside that directory create exactly one text file named  
        /home/user/backup_audit/backup_integrity_report.log.

      The report file has to be plain UTF-8 text *without* colour codes or extra
      whitespace and **must follow this exact format, one line per file**:

          <filename>: OK
          <filename>: MISMATCH (expected <hash-in-manifest>, found <hash-on-disk>)

      Example (do not hard-code these values):

          db.sql: OK
          photos.tar.gz: MISMATCH (expected deadbeef…, found cafebabe…)

      • The order of lines must match the order in which the file names appear
        inside sha256sums.txt.  
      • Do not write anything else to the log file—no timestamps,
        headings, blank lines, or shell-command echoes.

  2.  A minor security hardening is required:

      • Remove write permissions for group and others from the checksum manifest
        /home/user/backups/sha256sums.txt (it should end up exactly 0644).  
      • Remove write permissions for group and others from the directory
        /home/user/backups itself (it should end up exactly 0755).

  Altogether, the whole job can be accomplished with just a couple of shell
  commands plus a redirect for the log.  When you finish, the automated tests
  will check:

      • The presence and *exact* contents of
        /home/user/backup_audit/backup_integrity_report.log.  
      • That /home/user/backups/sha256sums.txt has permissions 0644.  
      • That /home/user/backups has permissions 0755.

  Do not delete, move, or rename any existing files.  Your answer here should be
  the terminal commands you would run to complete the task.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

  Directory /home/user/backups   – permissions 0777

      /home/user/backups/db.sql            (empty file, size 0 bytes)
      /home/user/backups/photos.tar.gz     (empty file, size 0 bytes)

      /home/user/backups/sha256sums.txt    – permissions 0666
          Contents (exact two lines, newline at end of each):
              e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  db.sql
              aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa  photos.tar.gz
          (The first checksum is the correct SHA-256 for an empty file, the
           second one is intentionally wrong.)

  No directory called /home/user/backup_audit exists yet.

  Expected final state after the agent finishes:

  1. Directory /home/user/backup_audit exists, default permissions (agent’s umask).

  2. File /home/user/backup_audit/backup_integrity_report.log exists with
     exactly this two-line content and a trailing newline after each line:

        db.sql: OK
        photos.tar.gz: MISMATCH (expected aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa, found e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

  3. Permissions:

        /home/user/backups                 – 0755
        /home/user/backups/sha256sums.txt  – 0644

  No other file or directory modifications are expected.
name: task_000000_83a5b5ca
